visual_attributes.morphology: Small primate with elongated hind limbs and large eyes
distinctive_features: Enormous eyes approximately 16 mm in diameter
Long hind limbs with elongated tarsus bones
Slender tail ranging from 20 to 25 cm
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Head and body length 10 to 15 cm; hind limbs about twice this length
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Vertical clinging and leaping morphology with elongated fingers

domain_specific_visual.EYE_SIZE: As large as or larger than the brain
domain_specific_visual.FINGER_LENGTH: Third finger as long as the upper arm

functional_attributes.primary_function: Nocturnal predator of insects and small vertebrates

domain_specific_functional.AUDITORY_SENSE: Strong auditory sense with distinct auditory cortex
domain_specific_functional.VISION: Unique cranial anatomy for balancing large eyes

relationships.description: Tarsiers are found primarily in forested habitats in Southeast Asia.
Philippine tarsiers belong to the genus Carlito.
Western tarsiers belong to the genus Cephalopachus.
